digest.component_or_area: MCP auth framework (composite audience choke point + static bearer verifier + resource-server wiring)
propositions: Adds flat scalar auth config keys to McpConfig with coercer entries, env-var registration, and regenerated reference docs, verified by manifest drift and round-trip tests.

Introduces a CompositeTokenVerifier that is the single audience and fail-closed choke point, re-checking every candidate token's resource equals auth_resource_server_url and treating a raising verifier as non-acceptance.
A config-driven factory over the closed strategy set (static|jwt|introspection|proxy|custom) refuses to start on unknown strategy, empty composite, or auth-enabled HTTP with no verifier wired.

StaticBearerVerifier parses the static tokens secrets file, stores only SHA-256 digests, compares constant-time, and stamps resource=auth_resource_server_url; records with plaintext literal or missing token_env/token_sha256 are rejected at startup.
build_server wires the verifier and AuthSettings into FastMCP so the SDK serves the RFC 9728 PRM and emits 401 + WWW-Authenticate; valid token yields 200, missing scope yields 403.

A redaction helper ensures no token or secret substring is emitted on any auth error or log path, and session-id replay by a different client without a valid token is never served the original principal's authenticated context.
